description: Human-authored frozen holdout ground truth for Knowledge Object extraction. Model execution has not yet been run.

objects.name: Composite Function
source_spans: The composite function \(h = f \circ g\) maps an input \(x \in \mathbb{R}^m\) to \(f(g(x))\).

objects.name: Chain Rule
source_spans: The chain rule describes how the derivative of the composite function depends on the derivatives of \(f\) and \(g\).

objects.name: Jacobian Matrix
source_spans: the Jacobian matrix \(J_g(x)\) collects all first-order partial derivatives of the output components of \(g\)

objects.name: Local Linear Approximation
source_spans: It gives the best local linear approximation to \(g\) near \(x\).

objects.name: Multivariable Chain Rule Formula
source_spans: \nabla h(x) = J_g(x)^\top \nabla f(g(x)).

objects.name: Gradient
source_spans: This formula shows how gradients and Jacobian matrices work together.

objects.name: Inner Product
source_spans: An inner product is an operation that takes two vectors and returns a scalar.

objects.name: Dot Product
source_spans: In Euclidean space, the standard inner product is the dot product.

objects.name: Orthogonal Vectors
source_spans: Two vectors are orthogonal when their inner product is zero.

objects.name: Orthogonal Projection
source_spans: Orthogonal projection is a method for finding the closest point in a subspace to a given vector.

objects.name: Projection Formula
source_spans: \operatorname{proj}_u(v) = \frac{v \cdot u}{u \cdot u}u.

objects.name: Least Squares Problem
source_spans: the least squares problem asks for a vector \(x\) that minimizes \(\|Ax-b\|^2\).

objects.name: Normal Equations
source_spans: A^\top A x = A^\top b.

objects.name: Subspace
source_spans: the closest point in a subspace to a given vector

objects.name: Random Variable
source_spans: A random variable is a function that assigns a numerical value to each outcome of a random experiment.

objects.name: Probability Distribution
source_spans: The probability distribution of a random variable describes how probability is assigned to its possible values.

objects.name: Expected Value
source_spans: The expected value of a random variable is a weighted average of its possible values.

objects.name: Expected Value Formula
source_spans: \mathbb{E}[X] = \sum_x x P(X=x).

objects.name: Variance
source_spans: Variance measures how far a random variable typically is from its expected value.

objects.name: Variance Formula
source_spans: \operatorname{Var}(X) = \mathbb{E}[(X-\mathbb{E}[X])^2].

objects.name: Conditional Probability
source_spans: Conditional probability measures the probability of an event given that another event has occurred.

objects.name: Conditional Probability Formula
source_spans: P(A \mid B) = \frac{P(A \cap B)}{P(B)}.

objects.name: Bayes' Rule
source_spans: Bayes' rule rewrites conditional probability in a form that is useful for updating beliefs:

objects.name: Bayes' Rule Formula
source_spans: P(A \mid B) = \frac{P(B \mid A)P(A)}{P(B)}.

objects.name: Event
source_spans: the probability of an event given that another event has occurred
